human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for housing analytics boundaries, metric-definition rigor, HMIS/HDX/IDIS/CHAS/ACS/LIHTC/HMDA/FHFA source handling, privacy and disclosure caveats, adjacent-authority routing, functionality coverage, and validated spec-pack completeness.

review_notes: All required issue scenario families and cross-industry research-contract scenario families are covered and mapped to passing results.
All required functional abilities for shared specialist baseline, analytics-reporting-and-disclosure, and cross-industry artifact awareness are represented and validated.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to housing metric definition, source hierarchy, geography and vintage handling, denominator and exclusion logic, HMIS/HDX/IDIS/CHAS/ACS/LIHTC/HMDA/FHFA reconciliation notes, caveat design, action thresholds, privacy-sensitive handling, and adjacent-authority escalation rather than generic BI work.

Authority sources cover HUD HMIS data standards, HDX HIC/PIT guidance, CoC system performance measures, IDIS, eCon Planning Suite, CHAS, LIHTC public data, FFIEC/CFPB HMDA, Census ACS, FHFA HPI, HUD CPD monitoring, and SEC KPI disclosure guidance.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal in-scope housing analysis, ambiguous affordability metrics, incomplete homelessness data, cross-system conflicts, privacy and low-cell handling, unsupported causal or policy claims, generic-overreach refusal, adjacent eligibility/compliance/finance/legal boundaries, terminology normalization, tenant adaptation, and conflicting public-source handling.
Runtime remains specification-based with explicit promotion requirements for any later retained-implementation transition.
notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains; tenant-specific housing metric definitions, jurisdictional rules, funder terms, privacy controls, local source precedence, geography crosswalks, and publication approvals are handled through runtime retrieval and escalation rules.
This package is ready for validated operations under specification-based execution controls.
